model:
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  false)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 2631486f1bf.c is at L33 (dfree(arr) called with potentially already-freed/corrupted arr when RECURSIVE=T). This alarm is PULSE_UNINITIALIZED_VALUE for head at L67: in main(), head is declared but never initialized; curr->next = head at the first loop iteration reads an uninitialized value — a genuine bug. This is a different, new bug not in the VBDB. The uninitialized read of head happens in all configurations regardless of RECURSIVE or SORTFILES (neither flag affects the main loop). The PC {RECURSIVE=F, SORTFILES=F} is unnecessarily narrow — the bug also exists when RECURSIVE=T or SORTFILES=T. Over-constrained.
